Notes

Short write-ups on things I'm figuring out. Written in Typst — try the syntax with Typstnique.

I write all my lecture notes in Typst — a modern typesetting system that compiles as you type, with real math, clean PDFs, and no LaTeX boilerplate. I have a shared temp.typ that every course imports: custom #theorem / #definition boxes, matrix shorthands like succ and mat, calculus operators like #dv(f,x) and #ppv(f,x), and a bunch of symbol aliases so KR proofs don't look like line noise. The demo below cycles through a few real snippets.

notes.typ 
 
 
 
 
  notes.pdf 
 
  
 
 
 
  KKT Conditions — A Quick Primer  optimisation · KKT · calculus  · 
Jun 2026
